title: Interactive Heating Curve Simulator
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Animated heating curve that compares four substances, highlights plateaus vs. slopes, and quantifies how energy is distributed across phase changes.

learningObjectives: Identify each segment of a heating curve and explain whether heat input changes temperature or phase.
Compare how thermodynamic constants (specific heats, enthalpy of fusion, enthalpy of vaporization) alter the slope and length of heating-curve regions.
prerequisites: Definition of specific heat and enthalpy changes
Ability to read temperature vs. heat graphs

keyQuestions: Which segment uses the greatest fraction of energy for water and why?
How does switching to ethanol or benzene change the ratio of plateau to slope lengths?
commonMisconceptions: Heating rate changes the shape of an energy vs. temperature curve.
Plateau temperatures depend on how fast heat is added rather than material properties.
assessmentOpportunities: Exit ticket asking students to justify a longer boiling plateau using enthalpy data
Quick-write comparing energy pie charts for two substances
